raw_consumer_statement:
CFPB XXXXomplaint Statement Barclay Credit Card Dispute Denial I am filing this complaint regarding a defective and misrepresented inflatable bounce house purchased using my Barclay credit card. Barclay denied my dispute despite substantial supporting evidence and compliance with all requested documentation. 
Reason for Dispute : I ordered a brand new inflatable bounce house from XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX. However, the item I received was used, defective, and not as described. Defects and discrepancies include : - The inflatable has another companys logo printed on it ( Evidence Photo # XXXX ). - It is covered in blue ink stains and footprints ( Evidence Photos # XXXX ). - It does not fully inflate, making it unsafe for children ( Evidence Photo # XXXX ). - The product does not match the photos provided by the seller prior to shipment ( Evidence Photos # XXXX ). 
Timeline of Events and Supporting Information : - XX/XX/2025 XX/XX/2025 : Contacted the original company repeatedly to request a refund ( Evidence Photos # XXXX ). - XX/XX/2025 : Escalated the issue to Barclay and opened a credit card dispute for defective merchandise. - XX/XX/2025 : Barclay denied the dispute without sufficient reasoning. - XX/XX/2025 : Barclay requested a supporting letter from a similar company confirming that the item was defective. I obtained and submitted this letter. - XX/XX/2025 : After being told the first letter was invalid because it lacked company logo and contact information, I submitted a new version on official company letterhead with contact details and screenshots verifying authenticity. - XX/XX/2025 : Barclay again denied the dispute. - XX/XX/2025 : I called Barclay and requested a detailed written explanation of why my dispute was denied. Instead, I received the same denial letter from XX/XX/XXXX, which contained no additional reasoning or findings. During that call, the Barclay representative told me that I needed to ship the item back to XXXX in order to receive a refund, supposedly per XXXX return agreement. I reviewed the sellers XXXX profile and confirmed that no such return policy existsthere is no return agreement listed anywhere on the sellers page. 
Request for Resolution : I am requesting : XXXX. A full refund for the defective and misrepresented merchandise. XXXX. Reimbursement of any interest or fees accrued while this dispute remained unresolved. I followed all procedures, provided all documentation, and responded to every request from Barclay. Despite this, Barclay failed to properly investigate and provide a reasonable resolution as required under the Fair Credit Billing Act.
